content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcer PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
October 6,2006
John Alden
790 Mission Avenue
San Rafael, CA 94901
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. ,4.-06-187
Dear Mr. Alden:
This letteris-in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the1Act,,).r
QUESTION
Is it permissible, under the circumstances you describe, to reopen your campaign
account for the purpose of receiving contributions needed to pay a bili reclived from the
Sonoma county Registrar of voters after your committee had been closed?
CONCLUSION
The Executive Director of this Commission has authority to permit you to reopen
your campaign committee for the purposes you describe. As explained below, it is also
permissible for you to pay the Registrar's bill from your own personal funds, without any
need to reopen your campaign account.
FACTS
You are a former candidate for the Democratic nomination for the 6rt Assembly
District' You lost i1lhe June 2006 pimary, and you closed your campaign committee
("Friends of John Alden") with its associated bank account on August l,20o6,believing
that all debts from the campaignhad been paid or otherwise discharged
After the bank account was closed, you received a'bill from the Sonoma County
Registrar of Voters dated August 22,2006, stating that the campaign still owed $1,357.10
for a ballot statement printed in the voter guides for that election.
I Government Code sections 81000 - glll4. Commission regulations appearat Title 2, sections 1g109-
18997, ofthe California Code ofRegulations.
e9


File No. A-06-187
Page No. 2
You are eager to pay this bill, but are unclear on the proper method for payment,
and for reporting the payment. Your preference would be toreopen the committee bank
account, accept contributions of your own funds and those of others, andpay the bill
from those campaign funds, reporting the payment under the rules that would have
applied to the committee had the committee not been closed. The Registrar suggests,
however, that you are required to pay the bill from personal funds, anl threatens a
collection action against you personally if the biI d not paid forthwith. But you are also
concerned that such a payment from personal funds maybe impermissible under the Act.
The Act requires generally that all of a candidate's campaign expenditures be paid
from a single bank account maintained for that purpose. (Section dszoi,copy enclosid).
But section 85201(D Td (g) expressly states that a candidate's canpaignbairl account
need not be employed when the candidate uses his or herpersonal funds for the kind of
payment you describe. Section 82025likewise exempts payments for ballot pamphlet
statements from classification as campaign expenditures.ifthe payrnent is made from a
candidate's personal funds. The position taken by the Sonoma -ounty Registrar, that the
cost of your ballot pamphlet statement need not be paid from your campaign account, is
consistent with the Act's treatment of such payments, when the candidat" !hoor". to use
his or her personal funds. However, the Act contains no provision requiringpayment of
such a cost from a candidate's personal funds. Rather, the Act permits such"costs to be
pud either from personal funds or from a campaign bank account.
However, if you wish to receive contributions to assist in paying the cost of your
ballot pamphlet statement, you must reopen your cofirmittee to receive the contributions
and make the payment. Regulation 18404.1(copy enclosed) states the rules under which
committees are terminated and may be reopened. With one exception that does not seem
relevant here, these rules apply to committees for state electiv" o-ffi"", and do not apply to
committees formed for local election campaigns. Since the committee you describe was
a committee for state elective office, Regulation 18404.1governs the piocess you must
use to reopen your coflrmittee, if you choose to make this payment with the assistance of
contributions.
ANALYSIS
As pertinent here, regulation I S404.1(i) provides as follows
"(i) Reopening of Terminated Committees. A committee
subject to the requirements of this regulation may submit a
request to the Executive Director to reopen for any of the
following reasons:
(1) To receive a refund or similarpayment received after
termination of the committee;
(2) To pay a fine as permitted under Govemment Code
section 89513(c);


File No. A-06-187
Page No. 3
(3) To pay expenses incurred in connection with an audit
or investigation of the committee under this title;
@) To pay litigation expenses as permitted under
Government code sections 99513 and g95r4, other than
expenses subject to Govemment code section g5304 and 2
Cal. Code Regs. section 1g530.4; or
(5) For any other good cause shown that would further the
disclosure requirements or contribution limits of this title.
Assuming that leave is granted, subdivisions (k) and (l) state the required
procedures for reopening the committee:
"(k) procedures for Reopening Terminated committees.
In order for the committee's reopening to be effective, the
committee must:
(1) Request and receive approvar to reopen the committee
from the Executive Director of the Fair Fohtical practices
Commission as specified in this subdivision. The
purpose(s) for requesting the reopening of the committee
must be included in the request. within 15 days after a
request is received by the Executive Director, the requestor
shall be notified in writing of the decision of the Executive
Director. In denying or granting the request, the Executive
Director shall consider whether the proposed purpose(s) for
reopening the committee are as specified in subdivision (i)
of this regulation, and specify the purpose(s) for reopening
the committee in the notification if the request is granted. -
If the request is denied, the notification shall state the
reason for the denial and advise the requestor of the right to
appear the decision to the chairman within 10 days aftL
the date of the transmittal of the denial notification. Any
such appeal shall:
(A) Be submitted to the Commission Offices either in
person, by fax, or by overnight delivery service;
(B) Include all materiar, if any, submitted with the original
request to reopen supporting any of the permissible reasons
for reopening as set forth in subdivisiorr(i) of this
regulation upon which the committee is basing its request;
and
(C) Directly address the reasons for the denial of the
request stated in the Executive Director,s notification to the
committee.
The Chairman,s decision shall be final, and may not be
appealed to the Commission.


File No. 4-06-187
Page No. 4
Q) File an amendment to Form 410 (Statement of
Orgarization) declaring the committee,s reopening with the
secretary of state along with written atthoization from the
Executive Director granting the reopening of the
committee;
(3) Mark "Amendment" on the Form 410 andlist the
committee's original identification number and name;
(4) Include the word "Reopened,, in parentheses after the
committee name on the Form 410; and
(5) File a copy of the amended staternent of organization
with the local filing officer, if any, with whom the
committee is required to file the originals of its campaign
reports pursuant to Government Code section 94215.
(l) Filing Requirements. A reopened committee is subject
to all of the committee filing requirements of this title
including those specified in subdivision (k) of this
regulation."
Because the cost of the ballot pamphlet statement is not an expenditure under the
Act when paid from a candidate's personal funds, you may find it -or" "orrrenient to pay
the outstanding charge with personal funds, uhich you may do without need of reop""i"g
the committee or indeed for further reporting. If you do wish to reopen the commiitee,
you must direct that request as specified in regulation 1840 4.7, to the Commission,s
Executive Director, Mark Krausse, atthe commission's address.
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
John W. Wallace
Acting General Counsel
_r,
By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is it permissible, under the circumstances you describe, to reopen your campaign
account for the purpose of receiving contributions needed to pay a bili reclived from the
Sonoma county Registrar of voters after your committee had been closed?

CONCLUSION: The Executive Director of this Commission has authority to permit you to reopen
your campaign committee for the purposes you describe. As explained below, it is also
permissible for you to pay the Registrar's bill from your own personal funds, without any
need to reopen your campaign account.
